org.arl.fjage.remote

Class Tunnel

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arl.fjage.Agent
      • org.arl.fjage.remote.Tunnel
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Runnable, ConnectionListener, MessageListener, Messenger, TimestampProvider

    public class Tunnel
extends Agent
implements ConnectionListener, MessageListener
    An agent that serves as a tunnel for messages between two fjage platforms.

    A tunnel can be configured as a server or a client. A server tunnel listens on a specified TCP port for incoming connections from client tunnels. A client tunnel connects to a server tunnel at a specified IP address and TCP port.

    Once a connection is established, the tunnel agent forwards selected messages between the two platforms. The `agents` parameter specifies the list of remote agents or topics forwarded through the tunnel.

    • Method Detail

      • init

        public void init()
        Description copied from class: Agent
        Called by the container when the agent is started. This method is usually overridden by a class implementing an agent to initialize the agent and its behaviors.
        Overrides:
        init in class Agent

      • shutdown

        public void shutdown()
        Description copied from class: Agent
        Called by the container when the agent is terminated. This method may optionally be overridden to provide a clean up before termination.
        Overrides:
        shutdown in class Agent

      • connected

        public void connected(Connector connector)
        Specified by:
        connected in interface ConnectionListener

      • connect

        protected void connect()

      • onReceive

        public boolean onReceive(Message msg)
        Description copied from interface: MessageListener
        This method is called for each message to be conveyed to the listener.

        If this method returns true, the message is assumed to be consumed and is immediately discarded.

        Specified by:
        onReceive in interface MessageListener
        Parameters:
        msg - received message.
        Returns:
        true if the message is consumed, false otherwise.

      • monitor

        protected void monitor(int id,
                       Connector c)

      • sendToRemote

        protected void sendToRemote(Connector c,
                            byte[] data)

      • removeConnector

        protected void removeConnector(Connector c)

      • getIp

        public java.lang.String getIp()
        Get IP address for the tunnel. In case of a client tunnel, this is the IP address of the server to connect to. In case of a server tunnel, this is set to null.
        Returns:
        IP address for the tunnel, or null if this is a server tunnel.

      • getPort

        public int getPort()
        Get TCP port number for the tunnel. In case of a client tunnel, this is the TCP port number of the server to connect to. In case of a server tunnel, this is the TCP port number to listen on for incoming client connections.
        Returns:
        TCP port number for the tunnel.

      • getAgents

        public java.util.List<AgentID> getAgents()
        Get the list of remote agents or topics visible through the tunnel.
        Returns:
        List of remote agents/topics visible through the tunnel.

      • setAgents

        public void setAgents(java.util.List<AgentID> agents)
        Set the list of remote agents or topics visible through the tunnel.
        Parameters:
        agents - List of remote agents/topics visible through the tunnel.

      • getTitle

        public java.lang.String getTitle()
        Get title of the tunnel agent.
        Returns:
        title.

      • getDescription

        public java.lang.String getDescription()
        Get description of the tunnel agent.
        Returns:
        description.
